wins.event: Net income $5.6B, +9% YoY; EPS $1.66; ROTCE 15.2% (approaching prior 15% target)
Investment banking fees +25% YoY; share gains of 120 bps in US IB since 2022
Asset cap lifted by Federal Reserve — strategic transformation milestone; total assets >$2T for first time
ROTCE medium-term target raised to 17-18% from prior 15% goal; CET1 target 10-10.5%
$6.1B share repurchases in Q3 (doubled from Q2); raised common stock dividend

risks.event: Severance expense of $296M ($0.07/share); FY expense guide raised to $54.6B from $54.2B
NIM declined 7 bps QoQ on growth in lower-yielding markets/trading assets
Commercial Banking revenue -9% YoY; CRE revenue -13% YoY
Office/commercial real estate losses continue and are expected to be lumpy
Possible additional severance in Q4 not included in current outlook (more efficiency actions in 2026)

guidance.notes: Long-term ROTCE target raised from 15% to 17-18%; FY 2025 NII reaffirmed at ~$47.7B with Q4 sequential acceleration to $12.4-12.5B; CET1 target lowered to 10-10.5% (more capital available for return).

_notes: Very strong structural call — asset cap removal + ROTCE target raise. Both pipelines correctly read this as bullish. The -3.4% realised excess_5d is classic sell-the-news after the asset-cap-lifting rally; not a sentiment-extraction failure but a feature gap (no pre-call drift visibility into how much of this was already priced).
